              MILITMY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMINGO.
*                      Executive Order Ns. 5 5 9 .

             A,meiidments of Articles Nos. 159 and 204, respectively,
                            of the Budget for 1920.
              By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
         of Santo Domingo, the following amendments and additions to
         the, Budget Law for the year 1920 are made and promulgated;
         Executive Order No. 357, published in the Official Gazette, No.
         3070,of December 6,1919;
              1. ?r,-rn x! aft:? Octcbcr 1 , 1023, the s4ary ol” one (1)
         pharmacist will be increased from $150 to $200 per {month,
         Article 159 of the Budget Law.
              2 . The person mentioned below is hereby authorized to
         draw a quarterly sum f m >the National Treasury at the rate
         appearing after his name, to be charged to Article 204 of the
         Budget for 1920,as a pension provided for by Executive Order
         No. 456:
              Jose Maria Septilveda . . . . . . . . . .$93.75 monthly.
    B         Provided that, in the case of this person, said pension will
         go into effect on November 16, 1920.

                                           THOMAS SNOWDEN,
                                         Rear-Admiral, U. S. Navy,
                                    Military Governor of Santo Domingo.

         Santo Domingo City, D.‘ R . ,
           November 10, 1920.
